title: USDC Binary Format Unit Contract

feature_decoding.minor_lt_10: all required Crate value types and sections used by AOUSD 1.0.1 readers must be decoded
feature_decoding.minor_ge_10: the version triple must be accepted, but value types introduced at minor 10 or later (Splines, Relocates, PathExpression) may emit a clear UnsupportedUsdcFeature diagnostic rather than be decoded

version_policy.rejection: version triples with major != 0, minor < 8, or minor > 12 must be rejected before any section interpretation

included: byte cursor with strict bounds checks
little-endian integer and floating-point primitive reads
Crate version validation for minor 8 through 12
non-owning section byte views into the opened resource
O(1) section addressing by standard section kind
deferred: mapping decoded sections into USD document specs
Crate versions with major != 0 or minor < 8 or minor > 12

model.OpenedResourceByteView: Non-owning byte view plus opaque owner of the opened USDC resource bytes. The decoder must not copy this opened resource to advance through sections.
model.ByteCursor: Target-native cursor over the OpenedResourceByteView byte view. Reads advance only after validating bounds and operate on non-owning bytes.
model.UsdcHeader: Magic bytes, version triple (major, minor, patch), and bootstrap pointer metadata required before section reads.
model.UsdcToc: Table of contents mapping Crate section names to validated byte ranges inside the same payload. TOC entries are read as raw uncompressed bytes (8B section count + N x 32B entries); only section-internal payloads (TOKENS data, FIELDS value reps, compressed integer arrays in FIELDSETS/PATHS/SPECS) are LZ4-compressed.
model.SectionTable: Validated mapping from standard section kind (TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, PATHS, SPECS) to byte views in the opened resource. Lookup is O(1) by section kind.
model.UsdcSectionBytes: Non-owning validated byte view for one table-of-contents section, sourced from the OpenedResourceByteView.
model.CompressedIntArray: Crate integer array representation decoded into target-native signed or unsigned integer vectors before higher-level section interpretation.

output.version: decoded Crate version triple (major, minor, patch)

output.sections: array of section name, offset, and byte size entries
output.sectionTable: O(1)-addressable SectionTable indexed by standard section kind

input.section: section name or section kind from a validated table of contents

laws: all multi-byte numeric reads are little-endian
all multi-byte numeric reads use a single little-endian word read; byte-at-a-time loops in the hot path are a contract violation
invalid magic bytes fail before any section interpretation
version triples outside the accepted reader range (major 0, minor 8 through 12) fail before any section interpretation
all table-of-contents offsets and sizes must lie within the opened resource byte view
integer overflow in offset, size, count, or decompressed-size arithmetic is an error
standard TOC sections are addressable by section kind in O(1) after read_toc
section bytes are returned as non-owning views into the opened resource byte view; copies are not permitted in the hot path
section bytes are returned only from validated table-of-contents entries
the binary-format unit must not expose a helper that searches the full opened resource as text, since such helpers exist only to bypass section traversal
this contract does not create USD specs, paths, tokens, fields, or canonical layer dumps

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with an ops array. Binary payloads are supplied as fixture paths or hex strings.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
